sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.106 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a residually finite$2$-group and let$x G$be a left 3-Engel element of order~2. Is$x^{G}$locally nilpotent? It is known that in any group a 3-Engel element of odd order belongs to the locally nilpotent radical (E.\,Jabara, G.\,Traustason, , 147, no.\,5 (2019), 1921--1927). Comment of 2025: An element$a G$is called a strong left$3$-Engel element if$a,a^{g}$is nilpotent of class at most$2$an...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
